summary: Large mncharsel decomp/matching PR centered on the character select screen. It added an implementation for mnCharSel_802640A0 and made many smaller matching-oriented rewrites in existing mncharsel functions, plus header/type corrections for CSS scene model/animation data and an HSD_Text field. The decomp.dev report showed main/melee/mn/mncharsel matched code improved overall, most notably mnCharSel_802640A0 from 0.00% to 86.22%, fn_802633B0 from 87.66% to 92.06%, mnCharSel_8025FDEC from 72.90% to 91.88%, mnCharSel_8025FB50 from 78.25% to 86.91%, and several smaller gains, though mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter regressed slightly from 100.00% to 99.90%. There was no PR body and no human review feedback in the slice.
what_changed: Added a large decompiled body for mnCharSel_802640A0 in src/melee/mn/mncharsel.c, including CSS camera/light/fog/model setup, text setup, audio entry SFX behavior, and related character-select screen initialization logic.
Added required baselib includes in mncharsel.c, including forward.h, cobj.h, gobj.h, gobjobject.h, gobjproc.h, gobjuserdata.h, lobj.h, and memory.h, reflecting newly typed HSD object usage.
Added local static GXColor constants mnCharSel_804DC580 through mnCharSel_804DC594 and macros MODELS and ANIM casting mnCharSel_804D6CB4/mnCharSel_804D6CD8 to typed CSSSceneModels/CSSAnimSet.
Introduced CSSSceneModels and CSSAnimSet structs in mncharsel.static.h to describe scene camera/light/fog descriptors and joint/anim/matanim/shapeanim sets.
Added static HSD_GObj globals mnCharSel_804D6CB8 and mnCharSel_804D6CBC in mncharsel.static.h.
Corrected CSSDoorsData.xd3 in src/melee/mn/types.h from float to HSD_Text*, and changed the corresponding static initializer in mncharsel.static.h from 0.0F to NULL.

Adjusted many existing mncharsel functions for matching, including mnCharSel_8025D1C4, mnCharSel_8025DB34, fn_8025F0E0, mnCharSel_8025FB50, mnCharSel_8025FDEC, mnCharSel_CursorThink, fn_80262648, and fn_802633B0.

Reworked several temporary variable types from u8/s8 to int or s32 while preserving explicit u8 casts at call/value boundaries, especially for handicap values, grabbed cursor IDs, player IDs, icon indexes, and ports.
Replaced some local pointer aliases to struct fields with direct mnCharSel_803F0DFC field accesses, notably scroll/page state in fn_8025F0E0 and door/team fields in fn_80262648.
Changed SisLib kerning/text usage to use typed pointers, including casting HSD_SisLib_803A6478 to TextKerning* and passing mnCharSel_803F0DFC.xd3 directly as an HSD_Text*.

Added target_funcs.txt listing matched/checkmarked mncharsel work targets: mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave, mnCharSel_8025FB50, mnCharSel_8025FDEC, mnCharSel_8025D1C4, fn_80262648, fn_8025F0E0, fn_802633B0, mnCharSel_8025C020, mnCharSel_8025DB34, mnCharSel_CursorThink, and mnCharSel_802640A0.
smart_moves: Typed previously opaque resource pointers with small local structs, CSSSceneModels and CSSAnimSet, instead of repeatedly indexing void* data. This documents the layout and gives future matching work stable names for camera/light/fog and animation bundle access.
Corrected CSSDoorsData.xd3 to HSD_Text*, which removed an unsafe float placeholder and made SisLib text positioning calls more natural.

Used wider int/s32 locals with explicit u8 casts where values originate from byte-sized game state but the compiler appears to keep them in full registers. This improved matches without losing evidence about storage width.
Preserved exact codegen-sensitive casts around gm_801685D4 and handicap fields, e.g. assigning `(u8) gm_801685D4(...)` into s32 temporaries and then comparing the s32 value to zero.
Avoided over-aliasing in some loops by using direct global struct field accesses, which can better match original compiler address generation than taking local pointers to adjacent byte fields.
Modeled the CSS icon table as `CSSIcon* icons = (CSSIcon*) (&mnCharSel_803F0A48 + 1)`, making repeated char_kind lookups explicit while preserving the data-layout relationship implied by the binary.
Reordered or moved local assignments only where the diff suggests codegen required it, such as moving temp_r30 assignment after temp_r29/var_f31 in mnCharSel_8025D1C4.
decomp_lessons: For Melee menu code, matching can depend heavily on the signedness and width of locals even when the backing fields are u8/s8; use int/s32 temporaries plus boundary casts when the original compiler likely promoted values.

Do not assume unknown data fields are numeric just because an initializer is 0.0F; cross-check call sites. Here CSSDoorsData.xd3 was used as an HSD_Text* in HSD_SisLib_803A746C calls, so the type and initializer were corrected to HSD_Text* and NULL.
Small wrapper structs around archive/resource blobs can unlock large functions by clarifying pointer arithmetic and reducing repeated casts.
Pointer aliases to global struct fields can harm matching. Try both local pointer aliases and direct global field expressions when nearby byte fields or floats are involved.
When matching menu/UI code, HSD text, JObj, GObj, CObj, LObj, Fog, and animation descriptor types often need precise includes and forward declarations before the C can be made clean and matchable.

Costume/icon selection logic may require loop shape changes for matching. In mnCharSel_8025FB50, the costume loop was changed to assign then test `isDuplicateCostume` inside a `while (1)` break pattern.

Array choice matters for codegen and behavior. The diff changed some model/cursor positioning references from mnCharSel_804A0BD0 to mnCharSel_804A0BC0 and changed a GObj link lookup to use `mnCharSel_804D6CF5 - 1` rather than `door`.
naming_conventions: New resource layout structs use CSS-prefixed descriptive names: CSSSceneModels and CSSAnimSet.
Existing symbol names retain project-address style for unknown or not-yet-renamed functions and data, e.g. mnCharSel_802640A0, fn_802633B0, mnCharSel_804D6CB4, and mnCharSel_803F0DFC.
Local convenience macros MODELS and ANIM are uppercase and cast global resource pointers to typed CSSSceneModels* and CSSAnimSet*.
HSD object types are kept explicit in declarations: HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_Text, HSD_CObjDesc, HSD_LightDesc, HSD_FogDesc, HSD_AnimJoint, HSD_MatAnimJoint, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint.
CSS gameplay/UI structs retain CSS-prefix naming such as CSSData, CSSDoor, CSSDoorsData, CSSIcon, CSSCharModel, and CSSTagData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use s32/int locals for promoted byte values, with explicit `(u8)` casts on assignments from u8-return-like functions or u8 struct fields.
Replace empty `if ((int) hval != 0) { } else { hval = 1; }` patterns with simpler `if (hval == 0) { hval = 1; }` where it still matches.
Prefer direct struct member reads/writes over local pointers to byte fields when trying to match scroll/page state code.
Cast HSD_SisLib_803A6478 return values to the concrete expected pointer type, here TextKerning*, before passing to helper functions.
Use typed resource bundle macros for globals backed by void pointers, e.g. `#define MODELS ((CSSSceneModels*) mnCharSel_804D6CB4)` and `#define ANIM ((CSSAnimSet*) mnCharSel_804D6CD8)`.
Model adjacent/static data tables with pointer casts when the symbol boundary is known but the table lacks a direct named symbol, e.g. CSSIcon table access via `(&mnCharSel_803F0A48 + 1)`.
Keep codegen-sensitive PAD_STACK usage tuned; mnCharSel_8025FB50 changed from PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(8) while adding an icons local.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR-body rationale were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased by +1600 bytes overall and matched data by +32 bytes.

decomp.dev bot reported mnCharSel_802640A0 improved from 0.00% to 86.22%, fn_802633B0 from 87.66% to 92.06%, mnCharSel_8025FDEC from 72.90% to 91.88%, mnCharSel_8025FB50 from 78.25% to 86.91%, mnCharSel_CursorThink from 89.29% to 89.87%, mnCharSel_8025DB34 from 87.80% to 88.76%, fn_8025F0E0 from 93.36% to 94.83%, fn_80262648 from 96.72% to 98.28%, and mnCharSel_8025D1C4 from 98.34% to 99.48%.
decomp.dev bot also reported one broken match in main/melee/mn/mncharsel: mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter changed from 100.00% to 99.90%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later mncharsel PRs for completion of mnCharSel_802640A0 from 86.22% to full match.
Check whether mnCharSel_8026688C_OnEnter's 99.90% regression was fixed in a follow-up.
Search for reuse or renaming of CSSSceneModels and CSSAnimSet in later menu decomp work.
Audit CSSDoorsData field names around xd3/xcf/xcd/xce now that xd3 is known to be HSD_Text*.
Look for future PRs that convert mnCharSel_804D6CB4 and mnCharSel_804D6CD8 from void* statics to typed globals.
